use_cases.title: Display the default weather map
use_cases.description: The user wants to open the app and see the weather map with its default configuration.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The app is loaded successfully.
steps: The user opens the main page of the weather app.
The app initializes the main map.
The app displays the default map view with the configured base layer and operational layers.
expected_result: The main map is visible.
The user can interact with the map.
The default map view is loaded successfully.

use_cases.title: Switch to the OpenStreetMap basemap
use_cases.description: The user wants to switch the basemap to OpenStreetMap to view the surroundings.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
The basemap-switcher (toc) is accessible and visible.
steps: The user selects the OpenStreetMap basemap in dropdown.
The app replaces the currently visible basemap with OpenStreetMap.
expected_result: The OpenStreetMap basemap is displayed.
The previous basemap is no longer active.
The user can continue panning and zooming on the map.

use_cases.title: Show the World Cities layer
use_cases.description: The user wants to display the World Cities layer to see city locations on top of the basemap.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
The layer-switcher (toc) is accessible and visible.
steps: The user enables the World Cities layer in the layer-switcher (toc).
The app loads and renders the city features on the map.
expected_result: The World Cities layer is visible on the map.
City features are rendered on top of the active basemap.
The map remains interactive.

use_cases.title: Display the legend
use_cases.description: The user wants to open the legend to understand the symbols used for the active layers.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
The World Cities layer is visible on the map.
steps: The user clicks the legend tool button.
The app opens the legend panel.
The app displays the legend information for the active layers.
expected_result: The legend panel is visible.
Legend entries for the active layers are displayed.
The user can inspect the map symbols.

use_cases.title: Display coordinates of a clicked location
use_cases.description: The user wants to click on the map and see the geographic coordinates of the selected location.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
The measurement tool is not active.
steps: The user clicks on a location in the map.
The app captures the clicked map position.
The app transforms the map coordinate to latitude and longitude.
The app displays the clicked coordinates in the location viewer.
expected_result: The clicked map location is highlighted.
The location viewer shows longitude and latitude values.
The shown coordinates correspond to the clicked map position.

use_cases.title: Show the town name for a clicked location
use_cases.description: The user wants to identify the nearest town or settlement for a clicked location on the map.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
A valid OpenCage API key is configured.
The measurement tool is not active.
steps: The user clicks on a location in the map.
The app sends the clicked latitude and longitude to the reverse geocoding service.
The app resolves the location to a town, city, village, district, or county name.
The app displays the resolved place name in the weather forecast panel.
expected_result: A place name is displayed for the selected coordinate.
If no detailed place name is found, a fallback name is shown.
The location information belongs to the clicked map position.

use_cases.title: Display the weather forecast for a clicked location
use_cases.description: The user wants to click on the map and view the weather forecast for the selected location.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
A valid OpenWeatherMap API key is configured.
The measurement tool is not active.
steps: The user clicks on a location in the map.
The app stores the selected coordinate.
The app opens or keeps open the weather forecast section.
The app requests forecast data for the selected coordinate.
The app displays forecast entries including datetime, weather description, temperature, humidity, wind direction, and wind speed.
expected_result: The weather forecast section is visible.
Forecast data for the selected location is displayed.
Multiple forecast entries are shown in metric units.

use_cases.title: Toggle the weather forecast panel
use_cases.description: The user wants to hide or show the weather forecast panel without changing the selected map location.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
A location has already been selected on the map.
steps: The user clicks the weather forecast tool button.
The app toggles the visibility state of the weather forecast section.
The user clicks the weather forecast tool button again to restore the panel.
expected_result: The weather forecast panel can be hidden.
The weather forecast panel can be shown again.
The selected map location remains unchanged while toggling the panel.

use_cases.title: Open and close the toc
use_cases.description: The user wants to show or hide the toc to manage visible layers and basemaps.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
the toc is visible
steps: The user clicks the toc button.
The app close the toc panel.
The user clicks the toc button again.
The app opens thetoc panel.
expected_result: The toc panel is hidden after the first click.
The toc panel is shown after the second click.
The map remains usable throughout the interaction.

use_cases.title: Measure a distance on the map
use_cases.description: The user wants to activate the measurement tool and measure a distance directly on the map.
preconditions: The user has started the app.
The map is visible.
steps: The user clicks the measurement tool button.
The app activates the measurement mode and opens the measurement panel.
The user interacts with the map to define a measurement line.
The user ends the measurement line with a double click.
The app calculates and displays the measured distance.
expected_result: Measurement mode is activated.
The measurement panel is visible.
A measurable geometry can be drawn on the map.
The measured result is displayed to the user.
